items.title: USDA posts updated list of SNAP food restriction waivers with 2026 implementation dates
items.summary: USDA FNS updated its SNAP Food Restriction Waivers tracker and lists multiple states restricting purchases like soda, candy, and energy drinks, with several target implementation dates in 2026. Examples include Florida (04/20/26) restricting soda, energy drinks, candy, and prepared desserts; Texas (04/01/26) restricting sweetened drinks and candy; and Arkansas (07/01/26) restricting soda and certain low-juice drinks plus candy.

items.title: USDA approves SNAP mass replacement benefits for California households impacted by Feb. 2026 winter storms
items.summary: USDA FNS notes it approved California’s request on March 2, 2026 to issue mass replacements of SNAP benefits due to power outages from winter storms that began Feb. 17, 2026. The approval applies to specified ZIP codes across multiple counties (including Placer, El Dorado, Fresno, Humboldt, and others), allowing affected households to receive replacement benefits without individual loss reporting.

items.title: CMS issues bulletin on Medicaid/CHIP managed care monitoring and oversight
items.summary: CMS’s March 12, 2026 informational bulletin reminds states they must maintain a managed care monitoring system covering at least 14 program areas under 42 CFR 438.66(b) (and 42 CFR 457.1285 for CHIP), including program integrity and network adequacy. The bulletin also reiterates annual submission requirements like the Managed Care Program Annual Report (MCPAR) due within 180 days after each contract year and network adequacy assurance documentation (NAAAR).

items.title: MACPAC March 2026 report recommends wage transparency reporting for Medicaid HCBS workers
items.summary: MACPAC’s March 2026 Report to Congress includes a recommendation aimed at supporting the home- and community-based services (HCBS) workforce by requiring states to report hourly wages paid to HCBS workers. The report also covers behavioral health in Medicaid/CHIP and Medicaid’s role for justice-involved youth and children in foster care.
items.source: Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission (MACPAC)

items.title: HUD updates Emergency Housing Voucher/Stability Voucher rules, requiring SSN and eligible immigration status verification
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 removes the prior waiver allowing PHAs to admit EHV/SV families without initially obtaining/verifying SSN or eligible immigration status documentation. HUD instructs PHAs to follow 24 CFR 5.508 for citizenship/eligible immigration evidence and 24 CFR 5.210–5.218 for SSN disclosure/verification, with PHAs required to be in compliance within 60 days of the March 5, 2026 publication.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: FEMA extends direct temporary housing for Hurricane Helene survivors through Sept. 30, 2026; phased rent begins after March 29
items.summary: FEMA extended direct temporary housing for Hurricane Helene survivors, allowing households in FEMA-provided units to remain until Sept. 30, 2026. The report states residents occupying units after March 29 must pay monthly rent on a phase-in schedule: 25% of HUD Fair Market Rent (April–May), 50% (June–July), 75% (August–September), and full HUD Fair Market Rent in October.

items.title: HHS/ACF proposes reallotment of $2.29 million in FY24 LIHEAP funds to FY25 LIHEAP grantees
items.summary: A Federal Register notice states $2,293,415 in LIHEAP funds from federal FY24 are proposed for reallotment. The notice explains the reallotted funds go to FY25 direct LIHEAP grant recipients (with certain exclusions such as very small allocations under $25), and that no subrecipients or other entities may apply for these reallotted funds.
items.source: Federal Register (HHS Administration for Children and Families)

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts; states must authorize at least one non-fresh form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts for fruits and vegetables: $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also notes the WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits/vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: IRS: most EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2, 2026; refund status updates by Feb. 21
items.summary: In its 2026 filing season announcement, the IRS says most refunds for taxpayers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) should be available by March 2, 2026 for filers using direct deposit with no issues. The IRS also states its “Where’s My Refund?” tool should provide projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: ED Federal Student Aid issues Dear Colleague letter on 2026–27 Pell Grant maximum/minimum award amounts
items.summary: Federal Student Aid published Dear Colleague Letter GEN-26-01 (Jan. 30, 2026) stating it contains the 2026–27 Award Year Federal Pell Grant Program maximum and minimum award amounts. The public page currently displays only metadata (not the award figures), so YoureOwed should verify the maximum/minimum award amounts from the official letter attachment once accessible.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)

items.title: SSA publishes 2026 federal SSI payment amounts (max $994 individual; $1,491 couple)
items.summary: SSA’s COLA/SSI payment table lists 2026 maximum federal SSI payment amounts of $994 per month for an eligible individual, $1,491 per month for an eligible couple, and $498 for an essential person. The same page notes the 2.8% COLA becomes effective January 2026.
